findings: Passed. The plate shows a single, anatomically coherent Red-shouldered Hawk: one head, one hooked bill, two wings, two yellow legs/feet, and one long, narrowly pale-banded tail.

All visible printed claims are supported: Buteo lineatus; Accipitridae; the stated length, wingspan, and weight; adult rufous barring, dark-and-pale checkered wings, black/dark tail with narrow pale bands, yellow cere and legs, immature streaking, and pale wing crescents.

The illustration agrees with the supplied adult field references in its rufous barred underparts, checkered upperwing, yellow cere/legs, hooked dark bill, and long banded tail. No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.

verification_sources.title: Red-shouldered Hawk Identification — All About Birds
